source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the physical architecture of a drone-soccer ball: an outer spherical CFRP lattice cage (12-20 connected rods + nodal joints) surrounds an inner quadcopter racing drone (center plate + 4 arms + 4 motor mounts + battery tray + flight controller) that is suspended inside the cage via cage-to-frame standoff mounts. No rulebook citations are added beyond those present in the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the spherical protective cage (drone soccer ball) for a drone-soccer 2024-25 rulebook benchmark. The cage is a spherical lattice of outside diameter 40 cm with a tolerance of plus or minus 2 cm (so 38 cm to 42 cm OD acceptable, 40 cm nominal). Inside the cage sits a racing quadcopter of approximately 250 g mass; the assembled ball including cage, motors, frame, battery, and props must still be compact enough to pass through a goal of inner diameter 60 cm at a height of 3-3.5 m above the arena floor. The arena is 14-20 m long, 7-10 m wide, and 4-5 m high. The cage must absorb repeated inter-ball collisions and ball-to-goal contacts during a match without permanent deformation. Specifically, the cage must survive a head-on collision between two balls traveling at 10 m/s each (relative 20 m/s closing speed, each ball approximately 500 g total) as a quasi-static 100 N impact equivalent applied at any point on the cage surface — no plastic yield, no fracture, and no intrusion of the cage geometry into the internal propeller disk (tip clearance to cage is typically 25-35 mm in standard 3-inch prop racing quads). The cage mass must be below 250 g (so that ball + drone total stays under 500 g). Typical cage construction is CFRP rods or molded carbon lattice. Primary structural pass/fail: (1) cage OD within 40 ± 2 cm; (2) propeller-to-cage tip clearance >= 20 mm at all points; (3) max cage rod stress under 100 N collision equivalent <= 0.67 x rod UTS (SF 1.5); (4) cage mass <= 250 g; (5) cage must recover elastically (no residual deformation > 2 mm after 100 N impact release). Primary verification is linear static structural FEA for the 100 N collision equivalent. Vibration (rotor-speed-induced) analysis is relevant to the inner drone but NOT required at the cage design gate. Thermal, fluid (prop aero), and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the cage, a mass budget, and a report listing cage OD in cm, cage mass in g, min prop-to-cage clearance in mm, max rod stress at 100 N in MPa, and residual deformation in mm.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The drone-soccer ball is a two-shell assembly: (outer) a spherical wireframe cage made of 12-20 pultruded CFRP rods joined at 6-20 nodal joints (great-circle or geodesic pattern) and (inner) a racing quadcopter consisting of a center plate, 4 radial arms, 4 motor mounts, 4 motors + 4 props, a battery tray, and a stack of flight-controller + ESC PCBs. The inner drone frame is suspended inside the cage via 4-6 cage-to-frame standoff mounts (rubber grommets or short CFRP columns). Load paths: LC1 100 N collision equivalent applied at any point on the cage surface propagates through 2-3 adjacent rods into the nearest nodal joint, then is shared around the cage; the standoff mounts transmit only secondary load into the inner drone frame. The prop tip clearance requirement is a geometric check on the deformed cage under LC1. The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model (cage + inner frame + standoff mounts + props indicated as clearance check) only; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part joint DCR at nodal joints and standoff-to-cage interfaces must be <= 1.0. All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original brief are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design a drone-soccer CFRP cage + drone-frame multi-part assembly for the Korean drone-soccer competition. Cage diameter 40 +/- 2 cm; cage mass 250 g; total assembly 500 g; minimum propeller clearance 20 mm to cage.

Load case: LC1 100 N impact at any cage node.

Pass/fail: residual permanent deformation <= 2 mm after LC1; no propeller strike under worst deformation. FEM at assembled level; strut-to-hub joint DCR <= 1.0.

prompt.title: Drone-soccer 40 cm ball cage (multi-part CFRP cage + inner quadcopter)
prompt.brief: Design a 40 +/- 2 cm spherical CFRP cage (12-20 rods + 6-20 nodes) around a 250 g inner racing quad (center plate + 4 arms + 4 motor mounts + battery + FC stack), suspended via 4-6 standoff mounts. Cage mass <= 250 g, total <= 500 g, prop-to-cage clearance >= 20 mm, residual deformation after 100 N impact <= 2 mm. FEM at assembled level; joint DCR <= 1.0.

material.typical_cage: CFRP pultruded rod or molded lattice
material.nodal_joint_material: 3D-printed nylon or injection-molded polycarbonate with rod sockets
material.inner_drone_frame: CFRP plate (center + arms), nylon motor mounts
material.standoff_mount: silicone rubber grommet or short CFRP column with thread insert

load_cases.name: collision_100N
load_cases.description: quasi-static equiv of 20 m/s closing at 500 g per ball

boundary_conditions.inner_drone_frame: provides internal 4-6 point support to cage rods via standoff mounts (G-parts)
boundary_conditions.standoff_mount: joins cage node (B-family) to center plate (C-family) via M3 bolt through rubber grommet
boundary_conditions.load_application: 100 N applied normal to cage surface at (a) the center of a rod, (b) a nodal joint, (c) the center of a cage opening -- three worst-case scans

objective.design_heuristic: Aim for 10-14 rods of 3 mm OD CFRP pultruded tube joined at 6-8 octahedral nodes to stay under 150 g cage + 80 g inner frame + 70 g battery/FC = 300 g total.

output_format.body_count: multi-body (cage rods + nodes + inner plate + arms + motor mounts + standoffs)

parts.name: cage_rod
parts.role: pultruded CFRP rod forming a great-circle or geodesic arc segment of the spherical cage; primary member carrying LC1 collision load in bending + compression
parts.count_note: Typical drone-soccer cages use 12-20 rods arranged in 3 orthogonal great circles (12 rods) up to a geodesic 2V icosahedron (20-30 rods).

geometric_constraints.curvature: arc segment of the 400 mm OD sphere

parts.material_hint: CFRP pultruded rod (unidirectional carbon, epoxy matrix)

parts.name: cage_nodal_joint
parts.role: 3D-printed or molded hub connecting 3-5 cage rods at a vertex of the spherical lattice; sockets hold each rod tangential to the sphere surface
parts.count_note: 6 nodes for a 3-great-circle orthogonal cage; up to 12 for a 2V icosahedral geodesic.

parts.name: inner_drone_center_plate
parts.role: central CFRP plate of the inner quadcopter; hosts the flight controller stack, receives 4 arms, and carries the battery tray underneath

parts.name: inner_drone_arm
parts.role: radial arm extending from the center plate to the motor mount; carries the 3-inch propeller and motor thrust/torque loads into the center plate
parts.count_note: One arm per motor; quadcopter = 4 arms at 90 deg spacing.

geometric_constraints.cross_section: 4 mm thick CFRP plate or 10 mm OD CFRP tube

parts.material_hint: 4 mm CFRP plate or 10 mm CFRP tube

parts.name: motor_and_mount
parts.role: brushless motor (e.g. 1404-series 4500 Kv) bolted to the arm tip via a nylon or CFRP motor mount; drives the 3-inch propeller
parts.material_hint: nylon or CFRP plate mount; commercial brushless motor

parts.name: battery_and_FC_stack
parts.role: 1S or 2S LiPo battery (approx 30-60 g) plus a stacked PCB assembly (flight controller + 4-in-1 ESC); contributes to drone mass but is not a structural member
parts.count_note: 1 battery + 1 FC/ESC stack (counted as 2 bodies in the assembly).

geometric_constraints.mounting: bolted or strapped to center plate C

parts.name: cage_to_frame_standoff_mount
parts.role: short rubber grommet or CFRP standoff column joining a cage nodal joint (B) to a dedicated anchor point on the inner drone center plate (C) or arm (D); isolates impact vibration into the drone
parts.count_note: 4 standoffs on the equator of the cage, aligned with the 4 inner drone arms; some designs add 2 polar standoffs.
parts.material_hint: silicone rubber grommet or CFRP tube column with brass thread insert

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: total ball <= 500 g with 250 g drone
Nodal joint (B) adhesive/socket DCR and standoff mount (G) bolt+grommet DCR must stay <= 1.0 under LC1 100 N collision at the worst-case impact location.
Under deformed cage at peak of LC1 collision, propeller tip clearance to nearest cage rod must remain >= 20 mm (not just at rest).
Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (cage rods) + solid (nodal joints, standoffs) + shell (center plate, arms) + tie constraints at rod-node sockets
recommended_fea_setup.contact_modeling: cohesive at rod-to-node bond; bolted preload + grommet stiffness for G-standoffs

interfaces.mediated_by: CFRP rod epoxied into a socket in the 3D-printed nodal joint
interfaces.load_path: LC1 100 N collision load enters at a rod midspan, flows along both adjacent rods into their shared nodal joints via socket shear + bending

interfaces.mediated_by: M3 bolt through nodal joint and rubber grommet into standoff mount
interfaces.load_path: Secondary LC1 load path: any residual radial deflection of the cage is reacted through standoff G into the inner frame

interfaces.mediated_by: M3 bolt through standoff into threaded insert on inner drone center plate
interfaces.load_path: Isolates the inner drone mass (approx 250 g) from direct impact; grommet absorbs high-frequency components

interfaces.mediated_by: 4x M3 bolt through arm base into center plate threaded inserts
interfaces.load_path: Motor thrust and torque from prop-arm-motor-mount (E) into arm D then into plate C; secondary reaction during LC1 asymmetric deceleration

fem_check_policy.rationale: The prop-to-cage clearance requirement couples the deformed cage lattice (A + B) with the inner drone prop disk (E on D on C) through the standoff mounts (G); no isolated per-part check can verify it under impact. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The drone-soccer ball rulebook explicitly treats the cage and the inner drone as distinct systems with separate mass budgets and separate compliance checks; the cage is a lattice of 12-20 bonded rods at 6-20 nodes that cannot be molded as a single part without losing the rulebook-preferred repairability.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact rod count and geodesic pattern are team-selected; common patterns are 3 orthogonal great circles (12 rods/6 nodes) or 2V icosahedral geodesic (20-30 rods).

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Prop aero not in cage gate.

eval_coverage: multi-part cage (rods + nodes) + inner drone (plate + arms + mounts) + isolation standoffs
nodal joint DCR and grommet-standoff DCR under collision

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
